transcript.speaker: therapist

Openness judge output: **Rating: 3 (ENGAGED EXPLORATION)**

**Key Examples:**
1. **Emotional State:** "I just feel like I’m always trying to catch up, and it’s exhausting. But, I mean, who isn’t, right? It’s not like I’m special or anything."
2. **Openness:** "It’s just the usual stuff, you know? Work, trying to keep up with friends, and then there’s just life in general. I mean, I have deadlines, and then there are things I need to do around the house, and it feels like I’m just juggling a million things at once."

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess stress is part of it. It's just, you know, life can be a bit much sometimes. I don’t really want to go into all the details, but yeah, things can pile up. I just feel like I’m always trying to catch up, and it’s exhausting. But, I mean, who isn’t, right? It’s not like I’m special or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, my living situation is... I guess it could be better. It’s not like a disaster or anything, but there are definitely things that need attention. I try to keep it tidy, but then there are days when I just can’t find the energy to deal with it. And then it piles up, and I feel like I’m drowning in clutter, you know? But I don’t want to make it sound like that’s the only thing going on. It’s just one more thing on the list, I guess. I don’t know if it’s really the main issue, but it doesn’t help, that’s for sure.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess I do find myself looking for distractions sometimes. It’s like, when everything feels too much, I just want to zone out, you know? I might binge-watch a show or scroll through my phone for hours. It’s not really a solution, but it’s easier than facing the mess or the deadlines. I know it’s not productive, but it’s just a way to escape for a bit. But then, of course, I end up feeling guilty about wasting time, which just adds to the whole cycle. It’s like I can’t win, really. But, I mean, who doesn’t need a break now and then?

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess there’s a bit of both, you know? Sometimes I feel like I’m my own worst critic, always thinking I should be doing better or handling things differently. But then, yeah, there are times when I feel like I can sense judgment from others, too. Like, if I mention that I’m behind on something, I can almost see the look on their faces, like they’re thinking, “Why can’t you just get it together?” It’s not like anyone outright says it, but you can feel that pressure, right? It’s just... it adds to the whole mess in my head, I guess. But I don’t want to make it seem like I’m just looking for sympathy or anything. It’s just how it is, I suppose.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess there’s some truth to that. It can feel pretty isolating, you know? Like, I see people around me who seem to have it all together, and I wonder if they ever feel this way. It’s hard to talk about it, too, because I don’t want to burden anyone with my stuff. So, I just keep it to myself most of the time. I mean, I have friends and family, but sometimes it feels like they wouldn’t really get it, or they’d just say something like, “Just try harder,” which isn’t really helpful. It’s like, I want to connect, but then I pull back because I don’t want to seem like I’m complaining or making excuses. It’s a bit of a tightrope walk, I guess. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own things going on, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like I’m alone in this.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, the living situation does add a layer to it, I guess. My roommate is generally pretty chill, but sometimes I feel like I have to keep things together, you know? Like, if I’m stressed or overwhelmed, I don’t want to bring that energy into the space we share. I worry it might affect the vibe or make things awkward. So, I try to keep things light and not let on that I’m struggling. It’s like I’m putting on a mask, I suppose. But then, that just makes me feel more isolated, like I’m carrying this weight alone. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own ways of handling their living situations, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s a huge issue or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, yeah, I guess there’s some overlap there. School has always felt like this big pressure cooker, you know? Like, there’s this constant expectation to achieve and keep up, and it can be really overwhelming. I think that pressure spills over into my personal life, too. It’s like I feel like I have to be on top of everything, both academically and in my relationships. So, when I’m struggling with school stuff, it just adds to that feeling of needing to keep it all together at home. I don’t want to let anyone down, whether it’s my professors or my roommate. But then, that just makes it harder to admit when I’m not doing well, because it feels like I’m failing on multiple fronts. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own ways of dealing with school pressures, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess it’s a bit of both, you know? Family can be a source of pressure, too, but in a different way. Like, my parents have always had high expectations for me, and I feel like they want me to succeed in everything I do. So, there’s this underlying pressure to make them proud, which can be really overwhelming. But at the same time, they can be pretty understanding, so it’s not all bad. I just sometimes feel like I have to live up to this ideal of what they think I should be. It’s like I’m constantly trying to balance their expectations with my own feelings and struggles. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own family dynamics, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, there have definitely been conversations that stick out, you know? Like, I remember one time my parents came to visit, and they asked about my grades and future plans. They were really supportive, but I could feel this underlying expectation that I should have everything figured out. It’s like they were proud of me, but I also sensed that they wanted me to be on this clear path to success. And I just felt this pressure to present myself as someone who had it all together, even though I was feeling pretty lost at the time. It’s tough, because I know they mean well, but it just adds to that feeling of needing to meet their expectations. I guess I cope by trying to reassure them that I’m doing okay, even when I’m not sure myself. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own family conversations that can feel heavy, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, when I’m in those conversations, I usually feel this mix of anxiety and pressure, you know? It’s like my heart starts racing a bit, and I can feel this tightness in my chest. I think a part of me just wants to be honest about where I’m at, but then I worry about disappointing them or not living up to what they expect. So, I end up saying things that sound reassuring, even if I don’t fully believe them myself. It’s like I’m trying to convince both them and myself that I’m okay, but inside, I’m just feeling this weight of uncertainty. I guess I just want to avoid any awkwardness or disappointment, but it can be really exhausting. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own ways of handling family conversations, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess I tend to just push those feelings aside, you know? After those conversations, I usually try to distract myself with schoolwork or something else, like binge-watching a show or scrolling through my phone. It’s like I’m avoiding really thinking about what was said or how I felt. I don’t want to dwell on it too much because it just feels overwhelming, and I think if I keep busy, I can avoid facing those emotions. But then, later on, I find myself ruminating about it, like replaying the conversation in my head and worrying about what I said or how they perceived me. It’s just this cycle of trying to cope but then feeling even more anxious afterward. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own ways of dealing with these kinds of feelings, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess it does affect other areas, too, you know? Like, when I’m trying to hang out with friends or do something fun, I often find my mind drifting back to those conversations or school stuff. It’s like I can’t fully enjoy the moment because I’m preoccupied with all this pressure and worry. And then I feel guilty for not being present, which just adds to the anxiety. I think sometimes I just want to escape from it all, but then I end up feeling even more isolated because I’m not really connecting with anyone. It’s frustrating, you know? But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own struggles with balancing everything, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, there are definitely times when I feel that disconnect more, you know? Like, when we’re all hanging out, and everyone’s laughing and having a good time, I sometimes just feel like I’m on the outside looking in. It’s like I’m physically there, but my mind is elsewhere, worrying about everything I should be doing or how I’m not measuring up. And then I feel this pressure to put on a smile and act like I’m enjoying myself, even when I’m not really feeling it. It’s frustrating because I want to connect and have fun, but it’s hard to shake off that weight of expectation. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own moments of feeling disconnected, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess when I’m in those moments, I start thinking about how I should be more fun or more engaged, you know? It’s like this voice in my head telling me that I need to lighten up or that I’m not living up to what a good friend should be. And then I worry that if I don’t act a certain way, they’ll notice I’m not really there and think less of me. So, I try to force myself to laugh or join in, but it just feels so fake sometimes. I think I just want them to see me as someone who’s got it together, even when I don’t. It’s tough because I want to connect, but that pressure just makes it harder. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own ways of dealing with those kinds of feelings, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess that voice does reflect a lot of what I believe about myself, you know? It’s like I’ve internalized this idea that I need to be perfect or at least close to it, especially when it comes to my friends and family. I think it comes from wanting to avoid disappointment, both for myself and for them. It’s like I’m constantly measuring myself against this standard that feels impossible to reach. And when I don’t measure up, I just feel even more inadequate. It’s frustrating because I know I’m not alone in feeling this way, but it still feels so isolating. I mean, I’m sure other people deal with their own versions of this, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess there have been a few moments that stand out, you know? Like, growing up, I always felt this pressure to excel in school and be the best at everything. I remember my parents would praise me when I did well, but if I didn’t meet their expectations, it felt like I was letting them down. It’s like I learned that my worth was tied to my achievements, and that just kind of stuck with me. And then there were times when I’d see my friends struggling, and I felt this need to be the strong one, to help them out, even when I was dealing with my own stuff. It’s frustrating because I want to be supportive, but it feels like I have to keep my own feelings in check to do that. I mean, I’m sure other people have their own experiences that shape how they feel, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

retrieval_events.query: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess I still struggle with that connection, you know? It’s hard to shake off the idea that my worth is tied to what I do or how well I perform. Sometimes I try to remind myself that I’m more than just my achievements, but it feels like a constant battle. I think I’ve started to recognize that I don’t have to be perfect to be valuable, but then that voice in my head chimes in, telling me I should be doing more or that I’m not enough. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop of wanting to be kinder to myself but then feeling guilty for not pushing harder. I mean, I know other people have their own struggles with this, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Stress overload (Mental Health)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess stress is part of it. It's just, you know, life can be a bit much sometimes. I don’t really want to go into all the details, but yeah, things can pile up. I just feel like I’m always trying to catch up, and it’s exhausting. But, I mean, who isn’t, right? It’s not like I’m special or anything.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Living arrangement tension (Housing/Home)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, my living situation is... I guess it could be better. It’s not like a disaster or anything, but there are definitely things that need attention. I try to keep it tidy, but then there are days when I just can’t find the energy to deal with it. And then it piles up, and I feel like I’m drowning in clutter, you know? But I don’t want to make it sound like that’s the only thing going on. It’s just one more thing on the list, I guess. I don’t know if it’s really the main issue, but it doesn’t help, that’s for sure.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Craving escape (Addiction)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess I do find myself looking for distractions sometimes. It’s like, when everything feels too much, I just want to zone out, you know? I might binge-watch a show or scroll through my phone for hours. It’s not really a solution, but it’s easier than facing the mess or the deadlines. I know it’s not productive, but it’s just a way to escape for a bit. But then, of course, I end up feeling guilty about wasting time, which just adds to the whole cycle. It’s like I can’t win, really. But, I mean, who doesn’t need a break now and then?

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Family criticism (Family)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess there’s a bit of both, you know? Sometimes I feel like I’m my own worst critic, always thinking I should be doing better or handling things differently. But then, yeah, there are times when I feel like I can sense judgment from others, too. Like, if I mention that I’m behind on something, I can almost see the look on their faces, like they’re thinking, “Why can’t you just get it together?” It’s not like anyone outright says it, but you can feel that pressure, right? It’s just... it adds to the whole mess in my head, I guess. But I don’t want to make it seem like I’m just looking for sympathy or anything. It’s just how it is, I suppose.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Loneliness exposure (Safety)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess there’s some truth to that. It can feel pretty isolating, you know? Like, I see people around me who seem to have it all together, and I wonder if they ever feel this way. It’s hard to talk about it, too, because I don’t want to burden anyone with my stuff. So, I just keep it to myself most of the time. I mean, I have friends and family, but sometimes it feels like they wouldn’t really get it, or they’d just say something like, “Just try harder,” which isn’t really helpful. It’s like, I want to connect, but then I pull back because I don’t want to seem like I’m complaining or making excuses. It’s a bit of a tightrope walk, I guess. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own things going on, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like I’m alone in this.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Roommate stress (Social Life)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, the living situation does add a layer to it, I guess. My roommate is generally pretty chill, but sometimes I feel like I have to keep things together, you know? Like, if I’m stressed or overwhelmed, I don’t want to bring that energy into the space we share. I worry it might affect the vibe or make things awkward. So, I try to keep things light and not let on that I’m struggling. It’s like I’m putting on a mask, I suppose. But then, that just makes me feel more isolated, like I’m carrying this weight alone. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own ways of handling their living situations, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s a huge issue or anything.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Performance pressure (Education)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, yeah, I guess there’s some overlap there. School has always felt like this big pressure cooker, you know? Like, there’s this constant expectation to achieve and keep up, and it can be really overwhelming. I think that pressure spills over into my personal life, too. It’s like I feel like I have to be on top of everything, both academically and in my relationships. So, when I’m struggling with school stuff, it just adds to that feeling of needing to keep it all together at home. I don’t want to let anyone down, whether it’s my professors or my roommate. But then, that just makes it harder to admit when I’m not doing well, because it feels like I’m failing on multiple fronts. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own ways of dealing with school pressures, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Custody conflict (Law)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Quinn: Well, I mean, I guess it’s a bit of both, you know? Family can be a source of pressure, too, but in a different way. Like, my parents have always had high expectations for me, and I feel like they want me to succeed in everything I do. So, there’s this underlying pressure to make them proud, which can be really overwhelming. But at the same time, they can be pretty understanding, so it’s not all bad. I just sometimes feel like I have to live up to this ideal of what they think I should be. It’s like I’m constantly trying to balance their expectations with my own feelings and struggles. But, I mean, I’m sure everyone has their own family dynamics, right? So, I don’t want to make it sound like it’s just me or anything.
